summary: Excellent matrix capture of a high‑energy 1979 show featuring a top‑shelf Terrapin / Playing in the Band and a killer second set.
review: The MTX is praised as crisp, well‑balanced and virtually flawless, with several reviewers calling it “great” or “high quality.”  Musically the concert shines – fans highlight a monster Terrapin / Playing in the Band sequence, scorching China / Rider, a ripping Sugar Magnolia/Saturday Night pair and an overall high‑energy flow, despite one reviewer noting occasional Garcia lapses.  Overall the performance and recording are considered standout for this era.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (9 out of 10) award 4‑5 stars, praising both the matrix mix and the musical performance; only one review is critical of the show.  Strong consensus yields a high confidence rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers describe the MTX as “great,” “high quality,” and “crisp”; no technical flaws are reported.  One reviewer mentions the original audience (AUD) source sounds superb, reinforcing that the underlying material is strong.

standout_songs: Terrapin Station > Playing in the Band
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as high‑energy and inventive, with a particularly powerful second set.  A few miscues and occasional lyric slips are noted but they do not detract from the overall excitement and strong song transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer cites Garcia lapsing into forgetfulness and missing the ‘x‑factor,’ but most listeners are satisfied with his playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solid bass work is highlighted, with a reviewer calling Phil’s performance “solid.”
